nodes.title: Epic: Tool surface curation: distribution, exposure & safety (DECIDE FIRST)
nodes.description:
Decide the TARGET MCP tool surface — keep / cut / gate-behind-flag for each of the 20 registered tools — and implement the chosen distribution model (lean core vs. opt-in management surface), destructive-op safety, and doc accuracy, BEFORE the per-feature protocol epics touch individual tools. Blocks the Annotations and Structured-output epics.

Spec: docs/tool-review-2026-06-18.md (initial 20-tool review). Overlaps the Annotations + Elicitation epics for the destructive-op safety model.

Model: TDD + validation-gated. 6 phase sub-beads run as a linear blocks-chain (P1→P2→P3→P4→P5→P6); each phase is blocked by the previous.

Epic acceptance criteria (verified at the P6 gate):
  - A documented keep/cut/gate decision for ALL 20 tools (decision record on the epic).
  - Exposure model implemented: lean default vs. opt-in (env flag) for management/CRUD tools, per the decision.
  - Destructive tools (delete_*) gated/guarded per decision (flag and/or explicit confirm) — coordinated with Annotations + Elicitation epics.
  - README documents the FINAL surface accurately (fixes the 12-vs-20 staleness incl. the 8 undocumented test-suite tools).
  - Any cut tools removed from the registry with tests updated; no dead handlers; version + migration note for the breaking change.
  - Decision recorded on the open gaps from the review (update_project thinness, search_* sort/recency, delete_environment).

nodes.title: [surface] P1 · Detailed design review
nodes.description:
Review the governing MCP spec section(s) and the CURRENT implementation. Capture concrete requirements, constraints, real-world client support, and risks. Deliverable: a design-review note on the epic + enumerated open questions.

Specific work:
  - WALKTHROUGH (collaborative): for each of the 20 tools record keep / cut / gate-behind-flag / merge, with rationale.
  - Resolve the contested calls from the review: delete_project danger, update_project thinness, the 8 test-suite tools, delete_environment, search_* sort gap.
  - Decide the exposure model: single lean set vs. core+management split, and what gates management/destructive tools.

nodes.title: [surface] P2 · Architecture analysis & design
nodes.description:
Analyze approach options, choose one, and produce the technical design: interfaces, type/schema changes, file-level change map, capability negotiation, backward-compat and rollback plan. Design is reviewed/approved before scoping closes.

Specific work:
  - Design the tiered registration mechanism (env flag(s) / build-time) and how tools/index.ts initTools filters by tier.
  - Design the destructive-op guard model (flag + explicit confirm arg + future elicitation) and how it composes with the Annotations epic.
  - Back-compat: cutting/gating tools is a breaking change for dependent clients → version bump + migration plan.

nodes.title: [surface] P3 · Scoping & test plan
nodes.description:
Decompose into concrete commits and author the TDD test matrix (unit + integration) plus the epic's measurable acceptance criteria. Failing-test NAMES are written here, before any implementation.

Specific work:
  - Concrete change list (tools cut/gated/kept). Test matrix: registry-composition tests for default vs. flagged sets; destructive-guard tests; acceptance.

nodes.title: [surface] P4 · Implementation (TDD)
nodes.description:
TDD: land the failing tests from P3 FIRST, then implement to green. No production code without a preceding test. Existing suite stays green; lint + typecheck/build clean.

Specific work:
  - Tests first (registry composition per flag; guard behavior), then implement tier-gated registration + guards + any removals (green).

(TDD: tests precede implementation.)

nodes.title: [surface] P5 · Documentation
nodes.description:
Update README tool tables, docs/, tool descriptions, CHANGELOG, and AGENTS.md as needed so docs match behavior. Add usage examples.

Specific work:
  - Rewrite the README tools section to match the final surface (the 12→20 fix); document exposure flags, destructive model, and the migration.

nodes.title: [surface] P6 · Validation gate
nodes.description:
VALIDATION GATE. Run full `npm test`, lint, and build; verify every epic acceptance criterion is demonstrably met. Gates epic closure.

Specific work:
  - Full suite + lint/build; verify default surface == lean set, flagged surface == full, destructive guards engaged, every decision reflected; close gate.

Gate verifies the epic acceptance criteria:
  - A documented keep/cut/gate decision for ALL 20 tools (decision record on the epic).
  - Exposure model implemented: lean default vs. opt-in (env flag) for management/CRUD tools, per the decision.
  - Destructive tools (delete_*) gated/guarded per decision (flag and/or explicit confirm) — coordinated with Annotations + Elicitation epics.
  - README documents the FINAL surface accurately (fixes the 12-vs-20 staleness incl. the 8 undocumented test-suite tools).
  - Any cut tools removed from the registry with tests updated; no dead handlers; version + migration note for the breaking change.
  - Decision recorded on the open gaps from the review (update_project thinness, search_* sort/recency, delete_environment).
